entry.name: EntityQueryResolve
entry.summary: Attempts to look up on the given entity, writing it into the given space if it finds it and the space was not already empty.
entry.remarks:
This is preferable to if it is erroneous for the component to not be present, but you don't want to crash the game.
This is also preferable if you may have already looked up the component, saving on lookup time.

remarks.para:
This does not return the entity it spawns, it tries to look it up, so if spawning that entity violates singleton status (or it lacks the necessary component) this will throw immediately after instead of later on next call.
This will also still throw if the spawned entity doesn't match at all.
This API does not obey . If you need to "pause" singleton entities, use an extra marker component that you remove when "pausing" them.
